Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations: There are three different patient demographics: (45, M), (62, F), (28, M).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Likely each is a distinct patient. Let's list events with their attributes and see which events belong to which patient.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Patient 1: age 45, gender M, insurance private (from event 1). Then event 4: Initial triage, age 45 M, triage_code=3. Then event 7: X-ray, age 45 M, body_part left_leg. Then event 12: Doctor consultation, age 45 M, diagnosis hairline_fracture. Then event 15: Patient discharged, age 45 M, followup outpatient_clinic. That's a coherent sequence: Register -> Triage -> X-ray -> Doctor consult -> Discharge. Also note department: ER for registration, triage, doctor consult, discharge; X-ray in Radiology. So that seems one case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Patient 2: age 62, gender F, insurance public (event 2). Then event 5: Initial triage, age 62 F, triage_code=2. Then event 8: Lab test requested, Dr. Smith, ER, age 62 F, test_type blood_panel. Then event 10: Lab test completed, age 62 F, results normal. Then event 11: Doctor consultation, Dr. Smith, ER, age 62 F, diagnosis minor_infection. Then event 14: Prescription created, Dr. Smith, ER, age 62 F, medication antibiotics. Then event 17: Patient discharged, age 62 F, followup pharmacy_pickup. That's a sequence: Register -> Triage -> Lab request -> Lab completed -> Doctor consult -> Prescription -> Discharge. Department mostly ER, lab in Laboratory.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Patient 3: age 28, gender M, insurance private (event 3). But event 3 registration is in Surgery department (not ER). Then event 6: Doctor consultation, Dr. Lee, Surgery, age 28 M, reason scheduled_knee_surgery. Then event 9: Surgery preparation, Surgical Nurse, Surgery, age 28 M, prep_type general_anesthesia. Then event 13: Surgery performed, Dr. Lee, Surgery, age 28 M, procedure arthroscopy. Then event 16: Recovery room, Recovery Nurse, Surgery, age 28 M, condition stable. Then event 18: Patient discharged, Ward Clerk, Surgery, age 28 M, followup physiotherapy_sessions. That's a sequence: Register (Surgery) -> Doctor consult -> Surgery prep -> Surgery performed -> Recovery -> Discharge. So that is a different case, likely a scheduled surgery.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus we have three distinct cases. The events are interleaved. So we can assign case IDs as: Case A: events 1,4,7,12,15; Case B: events 2,5,8,10,11,14,17; Case C: events 3,6,9,13,16,18. Check if any events overlap? No, all seem consistent. So answer: Group them accordingly.
